name: Flexport API
what_it_does: REST API for freight forwarder Flexport's logistics platform, enabling programmatic shipment booking, cargo tracking, customs document management, and freight rate quoting across ocean, air, and truck modes.
use_cases: Track shipments automatically through the full freight lifecycle — booking, in-transit milestones, customs clearance, and delivery
Generate and retrieve customs and commercial invoice documents programmatically for compliance workflows
Fetch freight rate quotes for ocean and air across routes to feed procurement or cost optimization tools
not_for: Small parcel or last-mile shipping (use EasyPost, Shippo, or ShipStation instead)
Casual or consumer-grade shipping needs without an active Flexport forwarding account
Self-serve sandbox exploration — there is no free tier or developer sandbox without a commercial relationship
best_when: Best when your organization is already a Flexport customer moving freight internationally and needs to build supply chain visibility dashboards, automated document workflows, or cost-tracking integrations against live shipment data.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need parcel-level shipping, don't have an active Flexport account, or need a self-serve sandbox to prototype without a sales engagement — the API is not accessible without a commercial relationship.

auth.notes: API key authentication with keys issued per Flexport account. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes — a single key grants full account access, which warrants careful secret rotation and storage practices.

pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing negotiated directly with Flexport sales. API access requires an active freight forwarding account — there is no self-serve or developer-only plan.

known_agent_gotchas: Shipment lifecycle has many state transitions (booked, cargo_received, departed, arrived, customs_hold, delivered, etc.) — agents must handle all states gracefully rather than assuming a happy path
Commercial invoice and packing list documents require exact field formatting per destination country customs rules — malformed documents cause customs holds
API access requires an active Flexport customer account that cannot be provisioned self-serve; dev/test workflows require a sandbox account provisioned through Flexport support
API keys have no scope granularity — a single key grants full account access, requiring strict secret management and rotation policies
Customs data is jurisdiction-specific; HS codes, duties, and documentation requirements vary by origin/destination pair and must not be assumed uniform
